title: senecio viejensis
intro: Senecio viejensis, also known as White Ragwort, is a flowering plant species native to the Canary Islands. It is a member of the daisy family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Senecio viejensis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine, particularly in the Canary Islands.
